query: Has the 7th TNM edition been updated to include biological markers like MSI-H or HER2 expression in early gastric cancer?

retrieved_docs.title: Gastric Cancer -- Histopathology -- Molecular Pathology
retrieved_docs.content: HER2 gene amplification : HER2 gene amplification is reported in 12% to 27% of gastric cancer cases, with protein overexpression in 9% to 23% of cases. HER2 positivity, more frequent in the intestinal subtype (33%) compared to the diffuse subtype (8%), has been associated with tumor invasion, lymph node metastasis, and poorer survival. HER2 testing is recommended for all metastatic gastric cancer, using immunohistochemistry scoring: negative for 0 or 1+, positive for 3+, with reflex fluorescent in situ hybridization for an equivocal 2+ score to confirm (see Image . HER2 Analysis)

retrieved_docs.content: M, Metastasis M0: No distant metastasis. M1: Distant metastasis present.

retrieved_docs.content: Identifying molecular drivers of carcinoma is increasingly important in determining prognosis and treatment. The Cancer Genome Atlas-based molecular classification [11] of gastric cancer incorporates molecular diagnostics and clinical and histologic features, including:

retrieved_docs.title: Gastric Cancer -- Histopathology -- Molecular Pathology
retrieved_docs.content: The United States Food and Drug Administration has approved immunotherapy for patients with microsatellite instability in solid tumors, including gastric cancer, allowing the evaluation of immunotherapy potential in patients with metastatic disease who have progressed on standard therapy. Epstein-Bar (EBV)positive gastric tumors generally have a better prognosis, although routine EBV staining is not yet recommended in clinical care. [12] [13] [14] [15] [16]

retrieved_docs.title: Gastric Cancer -- Histopathology -- Molecular Pathology
retrieved_docs.content: Microsatellite instability : Resulting from defects in mismatch repair genes, these tumors are hypermutated and typically found in more distal cancers.

retrieved_docs.title: Gastric Cancer -- Histopathology -- Molecular Pathology
retrieved_docs.content: Epstein-Bar Virus-associated : These tumors, located in the proximal stomach, are characterized by CIMP (CpG island methylator phenotype), mutations in PIK3CA, ARID1A , and BCOR , and amplifications of programmed death protein 1 and L2.

retrieved_docs.title: Gastric Cancer -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Often, gastric cancer is advanced at diagnosis due to the late onset of symptoms. Endoscopy is the diagnostic test of choice, allowing for visual inspection, tissue sampling, and resection of early tumors. [5] Gastrectomy remains the mainstay of curative treatment, with the extent of gastric resection tailored to tumor location and size. A comprehensive lymphadenectomy is also recommended. Patient outcomes improve with perioperative chemotherapy and potentially with radiotherapy.

retrieved_docs.title: Gastric Cancer -- Histopathology -- Molecular Pathology
retrieved_docs.content: Genomically stable : Genomic stability is associated with diffuse-type cancers, which have the lowest overall mutational burden, with mutations in CDH1 often present.

retrieved_docs.title: Gastric Cancer -- Staging
retrieved_docs.content: N, Nodes NX: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed N0: No regional lymph node metastasis N1: Metastasis in 1 to 2 regional lymph nodes N2: Metastasis in 3 to 6 regional lymph nodes N3: Metastasis in 7 or more regional lymph nodes N3a: Metastasis in 7 to 15 regional lymph nodes N3b: Metastasis in ≥16 regional lymph nodes

retrieved_docs.title: Pembrolizumab -- Indications -- Gastric Cancer
retrieved_docs.content: Locally advanced, metastatic gastric or gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma with tumor PD-L1 expression greater than 1% with disease progression after at least two lines of therapy, including platinum or fluoropyrimidine chemotherapy (and HER2/neu targeted therapy, if appropriate) In patients with previously treated gastric and gastroesophageal junction cancer, treatment with pembrolizumab resulted in an 11.6% objective response rate at a median follow-up of 5.8 months. [16]

retrieved_docs.title: Tissue Evaluation for HER2 Tumor Marker -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The human epidermal growth factor receptor-2 (HER2) receptor (previously called HER2/Neu) is a transmembrane glycoprotein with tyrosine kinase activity that belongs to the epidermal growth factor receptor family. These receptors are essential in controlling epithelial cell growth and differentiation. [1] These receptors are important, as aberrant HER2 protein overexpression has associations with some adenocarcinomas, including breast, ovary, endometrium, cervix, as well as lung, esophageal, gastroesophageal junction, gastric, and bladder cancers. [2] HER2 amplification or overexpression occurs in approximately 20% to 30% of human breast cancers. [3] This protein is strongly associated with increased disease recurrence and is a poor prognostic factor for survival. For this reason, HER2 is an essential target for the therapy of various types of cancer.

retrieved_docs.title: Gastric Cancer -- Staging
retrieved_docs.content: The American Joint Committee on Cancer/Union for International Cancer Control (AJCC/UICC) Eighth Edition 2017 staging system for gastric cancer (see Table. The American Joint Committee on Cancer Gastric Cancer Staging) stratifies 5-year overall survival (5-y OS) according to pathological stage and intervention (surgery only IA-93.6%, IIA-81.8%, and IIIA-54.2% or with neoadjuvant I-76.5%, II-46.3%, III-18.3%, and IV-5.7%). retrieved_docs.title: Gastric Cancer -- Surgical Oncology -- Endoscopic Resection for Early Local Disease
retrieved_docs.content: Lesion size : The lesion should be <2 cm in diameter. Histological characteristics : The lesion should be moderate to well-differentiated, indicating a lower likelihood of aggressive behavior. Clear margins : The margins of the resected specimen must be free of cancer cells, ensuring that the tumor has been completely removed. Absence of high-risk features : The lesion should not exhibit high-risk features, such as lymphovascular or perineural invasion. These features indicate a higher risk of metastasis and a poorer prognosis.

retrieved_docs.title: Gastric Cancer -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Gastric cancer is a significant global health concern, ranking as the fourth leading cause of cancer deaths and the fifth most frequently diagnosed cancer. The incidence varies by geography, with higher rates in East Asia and a general decline in other regions over the past 3 decades. Risk factors for gastric cancer include Helicobacter pylori infection, dietary habits, genetic predispositions, and age. The disease often goes undiagnosed until its advanced stages due to the late onset of symptoms, complicating treatment and lowering survival rates. Diagnosis typically involves endoscopy, which enables tissue sampling and resection of early-stage tumors. Treatment includes gastrectomy, lymphadenectomy, chemotherapy, and, in some instances, targeted therapies or immunotherapies.

retrieved_docs.title: Gastric Cancer -- Evaluation -- Staging Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: For comprehensive preoperative staging, chest and abdominal imaging are necessary to exclude metastasis and determine surgical resectability. [4] Early abdominopelvic computed tomography (CT) is performed to rule out gross metastatic disease. However, it has limitations in assessing T and N stages and detecting small peritoneal metastases, with an overall accuracy ranging from 42% to 82%. Biopsy of any suspicious solitary or oligometastatic sites should be performed, and paracentesis is recommended if malignant ascites is suspected. Chest CT is preferred over plain radiographs for better evaluation. In cases where initial staging is negative for metastatic disease, positron emission tomography combined with CT may be used to assess resectability. Serum markers, including carcinoembryonic antigen, cancer antigen 125, carbohydrate antigen 19-9, and cancer antigen 72-4, have limited diagnostic value and may be elevated due to other conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: Gastric Cancer -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: The prognosis of gastric cancer is closely linked to the extent of the tumor, including nodal involvement and direct tumor invasion beyond the gastric wall. Tumor grade may also offer additional prognostic insight. More than 50% of patients with localized distal gastric cancer can achieve a cure. However, early-stage disease constitutes only 10% to 20% of all gastric cancer cases diagnosed in the United States.

retrieved_docs.content: The entity “cancer of stomach” is linked to its parent concept “سرطان المعدة,” indicating that “cancer of stomach” is classified under the broader Arabic term for stomach cancer.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2711
retrieved_docs.content: Endoscopic dilation of esophagus is linked to the procedural category upper gastrointestinal endoscopy; biopsy, indicating that it is considered an alternative or related mapping of that endoscopic examination. Upper gastrointestinal endoscopy with biopsy is classified under the same category of upper gastrointestinal endoscopy; biopsy, reflecting its role as a diagnostic approach that includes tissue sampling. Together, these statements illustrate that both the therapeutic Endoscopic dilation of esophagus and the diagnostic Upper gastrointestinal endoscopy with biopsy belong to the broader domain of upper gastrointestinal endoscopic procedures, highlighting their complementary roles in evaluating and managing esophageal pathology.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2924
retrieved_docs.content: Upper gastrointestinal endoscopy with biopsy is classified as a combined procedure that includes both an upper gastrointestinal endoscopy and a biopsy, indicating that it encompasses the endoscopic examination of the upper GI tract together with the collection of tissue samples for histopathologic analysis. This integrated approach is used to diagnose and evaluate diseases of the upper gastrointestinal system.

By incorporating استئصال المعدة, the CTRP Intervention Terminology classifies this type of gastric surgery within its set of medical interventions, reflecting its relevance for documenting and managing patients who undergo stomach resection procedures.

Recognizing this finding is essential for guiding further diagnostic work‑up and management of patients with cancers where the primary site cannot be identified.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 2 -- Community 1228
retrieved_docs.content: The SIGN SYMPTOM COMPLEX is clinically associated with gastritis and duodenitis. Gastritis and duodenitis is categorized under the broader entity الِتِهابُ المَعِدَة (gastritis) and includes the more specific subtype الِتِهابُ المَعِدَةِ التَّآكُلِيّ (erosive gastritis). Recognizing that the symptom complex may reflect both the general gastritis condition and its erosive form aids clinicians in accurate diagnosis and targeted management.
